title: PR Review
description: Run a structured pre-merge review on any pull request to the universal portfolio + blog template. Covers correctness, security, accessibility, performance, type safety, test coverage, and documentation — producing a prioritised list of required changes and optional suggestions.
prerequisite: 09-test-generation complete. Run on every PR before merge.
instructions: Ask the user to provide: the PR diff or list of changed files, the PR description, and the linked issue or ADR if applicable.
Run each review category below in order. Do not skip categories.
Classify every finding as: BLOCKER (must fix before merge), WARNING (should fix, non-blocking), SUGGESTION (optional improvement).
Present findings grouped by category with file:line references where possible.
After presenting findings, produce a summary: number of blockers, warnings, suggestions, and an overall APPROVE / REQUEST CHANGES verdict.
Do not approve a PR with any BLOCKER findings.
If the PR has no linked issue or ADR for a significant change, that is itself a WARNING.

checks: Does the implementation match the linked issue or ADR?
Are all edge cases handled — null/undefined, empty arrays, missing config values?
Are async operations correctly awaited?
Are errors caught and handled — no unhandled promise rejections?
Does the feature work with all feature flags (showBlog=false, showWork=false, showContact=false)?
Does the feature work with minimal config (only required fields set in identity.ts)?
Are all new API route inputs validated with Zod before processing?
Are there any new process.env reads in client components (should be server-only)?
Are there any hardcoded secrets, API keys, or personal data?
Are any new external URLs rendered without validation via lib/validators.ts?
Are any new dependencies introduced? If so: are they pinned, audited, and necessary?
Does the PR maintain the Content Security Policy — no new inline scripts or unsafe-eval?
Are any new server-only modules correctly marked with the server-only import?
Does tsc --noEmit pass with zero errors?
Are there any new `any` types — can they be replaced with specific types?
Are there non-null assertions (!) without a comment explaining why they are safe?
Are new config fields added to the corresponding Zod schema in lib/validators.ts?
Are new MDX frontmatter fields added to the Post or WorkItem type and schema?
Does the PR include tests for the new behaviour?
Do all existing tests still pass?
Are there new edge cases introduced that need test coverage?
If a new API route is added, does it have integration tests for happy path AND error paths?
If a new component is added, does it have a component test and an E2E journey?
Do all new interactive elements have accessible labels?
Is the heading hierarchy maintained on any new pages?
Do new images have meaningful alt text?
Are new form fields associated with visible labels (not just placeholders)?
Is colour contrast sufficient for any new text or UI elements?
Is the new feature keyboard navigable?
Are new images using next/image with explicit dimensions?
Are new client components justified — could they be React Server Components?
Are new data fetches cached appropriately (generateStaticParams, revalidate)?
Does the PR introduce any new render-blocking resources?
Does bundle size increase significantly — is the new dependency worth its weight?
Does the change work for both technical and non-technical personas?
If config files are changed, is the shape backwards compatible — or is a migration guide needed?
Are new config fields well-commented in TypeScript with JSDoc?
Does the change respect the zero-infrastructure constraint (no new database, no new CMS)?
Does the change work with a fresh clone and zero environment variables configured?
Are new public functions documented with JSDoc?
Is the README updated if a new feature changes the setup or customisation flow?
Is docs/content-guide.md updated if new content frontmatter fields are added?
Is an ADR created or updated if the PR changes an architectural decision?
Are new TODO comments formatted correctly with phase references?

verdict_criteria.APPROVE: Zero BLOCKER findings. All existing tests pass. tsc --noEmit clean.
verdict_criteria.REQUEST_CHANGES: One or more BLOCKER findings. List all blockers clearly at the top of the review.
verdict_criteria.COMMENT: No blockers but multiple WARNINGs that should be addressed in a follow-up issue.

review_output_format.header:
## PR Review — [PR Title]
**Verdict:** APPROVE | REQUEST CHANGES | COMMENT
**Blockers:** N | **Warnings:** N | **Suggestions:** N
review_output_format.finding_format: [BLOCKER|WARNING|SUGGESTION] `file:line` — Description of issue and recommended fix.

outputs: List of blockers that must be resolved before merge
List of follow-up issues to open for non-blocking findings
guardrails: Never approve a PR with a hardcoded secret — this is always a BLOCKER.
Never approve a PR where tsc --noEmit fails — type errors are always BLOCKERs.
Never skip the Template Compatibility category — it is the unique constraint of this project.
Never leave a finding without a recommended fix — unhelpful reviews block progress.
A PR that only adds tests is always APPROVE-worthy if tests are correct and passing.
